templates.name: Project on hold
templates.body:
Dear {CLIENT_NAME},

Please be advised that due to our current workloads, we are prioritising projects where customers have responded to RFIs, approved our production drawings and require an "as soon as possible" delivery.

As we are still awaiting information/approval to proceed from you, your project will be put on hold until you are ready to proceed, but this will mean that delivery is unlikely to be sooner than {DELIVERY_ESTIMATE}.

Naturally, we will always try to work with our clients and improve delivery dates where we can.

templates.name: Capacity reached
templates.body:
Dear {CLIENT_NAME},

Just to let you know, Longden Doors has currently reached capacity for orders that have not yet been released into production. Normally we try and maintain an 8–10 week lead time, but with the volume of orders we have, this is no longer possible.

We still need to receive sign-off ready for production, which will assign priority to jobs, but our current workload means that production probably wouldn't commence until {PRODUCTION_START}, with deliveries being in {DELIVERY_PERIOD}.

We will always try to work with our clients and improve delivery dates where we can.

templates.name: Factory capacity (casual)
templates.subject: Factory capacity — securing your production slot
templates.body:
Hi,

Just a quick heads-up on factory scheduling. We currently have some capacity available, but we have several large jobs about to land that will take us into our seasonal busy period. Once they're confirmed, production slots will be allocated on a first come, first served basis.

It's also worth flagging that we have a number of factory operatives taking annual leave over the coming weeks, which will naturally tighten our available production capacity further.

If you're in a position to sign off drawings, return revisions, or complete checks, getting these back to us sooner rather than later will secure your slot and protect your delivery dates. I completely appreciate that sign-off and revisions take the time they take — this is simply to give you the best possible visibility so nothing slips.

Happy to talk through any outstanding items if that would help move things along.

templates.name: Factory capacity (formal)
templates.subject: Factory production scheduling — advance notice
templates.body:
Dear {CLIENT_NAME},

I am writing to provide advance notice regarding factory production scheduling over the coming weeks.

At present we have capacity available within our manufacturing facility. However, we are anticipating the confirmation of several large-scale projects in the near future, which will coincide with our seasonal peak in demand. Once these projects are confirmed, production slots will necessarily be allocated on a first come, first served basis.

In addition, a number of our factory operatives will be taking scheduled annual leave during this period, which will further reduce our available production capacity. We would therefore ask that this be taken into consideration when planning your project timelines.

With this in mind, I would encourage you to finalise any outstanding drawing approvals, revisions, or checks at your earliest convenience. Returning these promptly will allow us to secure your place in the production schedule and safeguard your intended delivery dates. I fully appreciate that the approval and revision process requires due time and diligence; the purpose of this message is simply to give you clear visibility of our scheduling so that your project is not adversely affected.

Should it be helpful, I would be glad to arrange a call to discuss any outstanding items and assist in progressing matters.

Thank you for your continued business.

templates.name: Post-deposit information request
templates.body:
Good afternoon {CLIENT_NAME},

Thank you for the deposit.

Before we can begin preparing drawings, we kindly require the following information:

- Final dimensions of structural openings and wall depths
- Ironmongery schedule — we understand you may be preparing hinge pockets for free issue hinges in each door set. We have allowed for 1 mortice per leaf for the latch/lock; please confirm if this is correct. If so, please send samples so we can incorporate them into our technical drawings.
- Latest revision of relevant drawings (floor plans, etc.)
- Door handings
- Delivery dates and any restrictions
- Final finishes to door sets (if different from the quotation)
- Any further changes or amendments not previously quoted

Will you be the primary point of contact for this project, or should we liaise with someone else?

If you have any questions or need clarification, please don't hesitate to get in touch.

templates.name: Schedule / drawing update
templates.body:
Good afternoon {CLIENT_NAME},

Please see attached the updated schedules and drawings for {PROJECT_DESCRIPTION}.

Any further amendments or queries please let me know.

If you are ready to proceed into production, we require you to check through both the schedules and the drawings to ensure everything is correct. If so, we will then need confirmation from you that you are ready to proceed into production.

Please note, these final checks are in place as standard practice to ensure the project is correct. Once in production, any further changes will impact delivery dates and may increase the cost of the project.

templates.name: RFI — start drawings
templates.body:
Providing all outstanding RFIs are addressed, we can make a start — even if the guidance is just TBC with an estimated date, we can draft our Rev A drawings.

Please be advised, we aim to keep our drawing revisions to a minimum. Further revisions will only be drafted once all RFIs are responded to.

We are currently advising an 8–12 week production lead time from full and final drawing sign-off. Any delay in information will result in this lead time being pushed back and being subject to change depending on factory capacity at time of sign-off.

templates.name: Production sign-off / scheduling
templates.body:
Dear {CLIENT_NAME},

Please see attached the approved Rev {REV_NUMBER} drawings. I will schedule these for production today.

As part of our production process, our drawing package undergoes a triple-check process. Once all checks have been conducted, our production manager will be in touch to advise on a delivery date.

Our current anticipated lead time is 10–14 weeks.

Please note:

- Our factory primed doors will need to be de-nibbed prior to painting. We recommend that all decorative work (painting, plastering, etc.) is completed at least 2 weeks prior to doors being hung to limit moisture exposure.
- We advise that no further changes be made once a job has been released to the factory. If changes are necessary, the full phase must be pulled from production until full sign-off is achieved, at which point the job will re-enter the production line at the back of the queue.
- All completion and delivery dates are estimates of the earliest delivery date based on our 10–14 week manufacturing lead time. Fluctuation is dependent upon current factory capacity.

templates.name: Delivery delay
templates.body:
Hi {CLIENT_NAME},

Just to update you, unfortunately your delivery date will now be week commencing {NEW_DELIVERY_DATE}.

We haven't yet received {OUTSTANDING_ITEM}, which has delayed production. I completely understand how important this is for your schedule, and I'm really sorry for the inconvenience this causes.

Please rest assured that if there's any way we can pull this forward, we absolutely will. I'll keep you updated as soon as there's any movement on the {OUTSTANDING_ITEM}.

Thank you for your patience and understanding — apologies once again.

templates.name: Small enquiry decline
templates.body:
Dear {CLIENT_NAME},

Thank you for your enquiry.

Unfortunately, due to the small nature of this enquiry (usually 10+ for us), I must regretfully decline. As a bespoke manufacturer, small enquiries have an adverse impact upon our estimating, scheduling and production.

I hope you understand and look forward to hearing from you again.

templates.name: NCR / complaint response
templates.body:
Dear {CLIENT_NAME},

Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We're sorry to hear about the difficulties you're experiencing and appreciate the detailed explanation.

Our records show that {SPECIFICATION_DETAIL}, and production was carried out strictly in accordance with those approved drawings. Once drawings are formally approved, they become the basis for manufacture, and any changes would need to be addressed prior to production.

As per our standard procedure, any Non-Conformance Report (NCR) should be raised within 48 hours of receipt of goods.

At Longden, we take full responsibility when errors occur on our part. In this case, the doors were manufactured to the signed-off specification.

Please note that current lead times for new production are approximately {LEAD_TIME} weeks from final sign-off.

If you would like to discuss this further or explore possible solutions, please let us know.

placeholders.label: Specification detail
placeholders.hint: e.g. the drawings were signed off for 51mm doors

templates.name: Acclimatisation instructions
templates.body:
Solid timber doors should be acclimatised for 7–12 days in their final environment before installation to minimise movement caused by timber's natural response to humidity and temperature changes. Store doors flat on level bearers in a dry, ventilated area away from direct heat, sunlight, and damp conditions. Never lean rail and stile doors up against the wall.

Keep wrapping on until installation, remove straps before hanging, and ensure all faces and edges are properly sealed. Allow clearance for seasonal expansion, and use moisture meters to confirm suitable moisture content. Avoid directing HVAC vents at doors or exposing them to extreme dryness or humidity.

In wet rooms, use engineered or moisture-resistant alternatives, or ensure excellent sealing and ventilation if solid timber must be used. Even after acclimatisation, some seasonal movement is normal, with doors generally stabilising after about a year.

templates.name: Fire door — wall construction
templates.body:
Supporting constructions may be timber stud, steel stud plasterboard partitions, blockwork, brickwork, or concrete walls, provided they have been tested or assessed to provide at least the same fire rating (30 minutes) when incorporating door openings.

Fire doors are tested as door assemblies only; the supporting wall is simply a known, rated substrate. The FoA confirms that the wall itself isn't what's being tested — only that it must already provide an equivalent or greater fire rating.

Our doors are typically tested in blockwork or stud partitions, but this does not restrict installation to those exact constructions. The supporting partition just needs to achieve at least the same rating (30 minutes) and maintain the fire line for the required duration.

In short: the certification applies to the doorset, and any correctly fire-rated partition that maintains the fire line is suitable, provided the interface is properly fire-stopped. We do not certify wall constructions — only the doorset performance.

If you need any further information on the doorset certification or installation details, we're happy to help.

templates.name: Porgies sad email
templates.body:
Good Morning/Afternoon {Client_name},
 
Please see attached the quote for {insert_project}.
Breakdowns of the cost are included on the alpha schedule.
 
Any questions/ amendments please do not hesitate to reach out.
 
Kind regards,
